channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Moldovans grapple with outages as energy crisis grips pro-Russia region
item.description: COPANCA, Moldova (AP) — Every time planned electrical outages are imposed on his village in southern Moldova, 73-year-old retiree Vasili Donici passes the time solving crosswords and puzzles in a room he illuminates with a small gas lamp.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and AUREL OBREJA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Korea’s acting leader accepts resignation of presidential security chief
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s acting leader on Friday accepted the resignation of the chief of the presidential security service, Park Jong-joon, as he faced police questioning over how his forces blocked law enforcement efforts to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol last week.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: LA area's 2 biggest blazes burn at least 10,000 structures, while new fire leads to more evacuations
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The two biggest wildfires ravaging the Los Angeles area have killed at least 10 people and burned more than 10,000 homes and other structures, officials said as they urged more people to heed evacuation orders after a new blaze ignited and quickly grew.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tanker hit by Houthi rebels that threatened a Red Sea oil spill has been salvaged
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — An oil tanker that burned for weeks in the Red Sea after being attacked by Yemen's Houthi rebels and threatening a massive oil spill has been salvaged, a security firm said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How to donate to LA wildfire relief
item.description: A swarm of winter wildfires ravaged America's most heavily-populated county to start 2025, scorching cars, banks, bungalows and multi-million dollar mansions.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: 7 notable moments between VIPs at Carter’s funeral
item.description: Former President Carter’s funeral service at Washington National Cathedral brought together all five living presidents and current and former officials for an event full of notable interactions.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about thousands of evacuations and homes burned in Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — At least 10 people were killed and thousands of structures were burned as fierce wildfires raged in the Los Angeles area, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What ignited the deadly California wildfires? Investigators consider an array of possibilities
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Investigators are considering an array of possible ignition sources for the huge fires that have killed at least 10 people and destroyed thousands of homes and businesses in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: JASON DEAREN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Winter storm plods into the Deep South, prompting states of emergency and school closures
item.description: Another blast of winter storms is closing schools, snarling flights and putting millions of residents on alert across parts of the Deep South and south-central U.S. The National Weather Service says heavy snowfall and icy conditions are likely Friday in Mississippi, Georgia, Kentucky and Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle And Kendria Lafleur, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google Trends: Nearly every US state is searching the Broncos over the Bills
item.description: We looked at Google Trends statistics to see whether the Broncos or the Bills were the talk of the country – and one team won by a landslide.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, Fox 5

item.title: Anita Bryant, anti-gay rights activist and singer, dead at 84
item.description: Anita Bryant Dry, known popularly Anita Bryant — a singer and crusader against gay rights — has died at the age of 84.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, NewsNation

item.title: The Los Angeles landmarks from film, TV damaged by wildfires
item.description: The high school where Brian De Palma brought Stephen King’s “Carrie” to life, Will Rogers’ ranch house, and a motel owned by William Randolph Hearst are among some of the famous structures that have been damaged or destroyed by the California wildfires.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: As wildfires burn in LA, what to know about protective masks
item.description: Certain masks are effective in protecting against wildfire smoke — but only if they are fitted and worn properly.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A powerful winter storm's heavy snow and sleet cancels flights and closes schools across US South
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A powerful winter storm brought heavy snow and icy conditions to parts of the U.S. South on Thursday, forcing officials to close schools, cancel flights and warn residents in some of the worst-hit areas to stay off roads as it lumbered eastward through Oklahoma and Texas. Arkansas Gov.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and KENDRIA LAFLEUR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge scraps Biden's Title IX rules, reversing expansion of protections for LGBTQ+ students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration’s Title IX rules expanding protections for LGBTQ+ students have been struck down nationwide after a federal judge in Kentucky found they overstepped the president’s authority. In a decision issued Thursday, U.S. District Judge Danny C.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lisa Kudrow says Matthew Perry left a note for her inside a recognizable 'Friends' prop
item.description: "I hadn't opened it up or looked inside it, but yeah he did. He had a note in there and I forgot about it," Kudrow said.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, Fox 5

item.title: Monkey found after California traffic stop is getting a new home
item.description: "The driver had a baby monkey attached - or clenched - on his person. That's something you don't see every day.
item.dc:creator: Briana Byus, NewsNation

item.title: Photos capture wildfires ravaging Southern California
item.description: All around Los Angeles, scenes of flames were everywhere, as many areas continued to burn all but unabated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House Republican bill would pave way for Trump to acquire Panama Canal for US
item.description: Rep. Dusty Johnson (R-S.D.) will introduce a bill that would authorize the president to purchase the Panama Canal and put it under U.S. control, an acquisition that President-elect Trump has been pushing over the last several weeks.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: ICE looking to expand migrant detention facilities across country, ACLU says
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union says information it has received from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement indicates that several migrant detention facilities on the South Texas border are among areas where the agency are seeking information to possibly expand under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires leave hundreds of thousands without power
item.description: An outage map provided by the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) showed just how widespread the outages extended
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Wildfires latest: At least 10,000 structures have burned in a series of fires around Los Angeles
item.description: Firefighters are battling to control a series of major fires in the Los Angeles area that have killed seven people, burned at least 10,000 structures from the Pacific Coast to Pasadena and sent thousands of people frantically fleeing their homes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: LIVE: More evacuations ordered after fast-moving Kenneth fire ignites in Los Angeles area
item.description: Firefighters are racing to contain a new wildfire that broke out close to homes in the area of Calabasas and Hidden Hills, northwest of Los Angeles, on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter will be honored at Washington funeral before burial in Georgia hometown
item.description: Jimmy Carter, who considered himself an outsider even as he sat in the Oval Office as the 39th U.S. president, will be honored Thursday with the pageantry of a funeral at Washington National Cathedral before a second service and burial in his tiny Georgia hometown.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Capitol Police arrest man attempting to set his car on fire amid Trump DC visit with GOP senators
item.description: The U.S. Capitol Police (USCP) said that they arrested a person who attempted to set his car near the Capitol building during President-elect Trump's visit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jimmy Carter's life intersected with slavery's legacy. His record on Civil Rights is complicated
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Young Jimmy Carter and his friends were walking across a pasture after a day’s farm labor during the Great Depression. As they came to a gate, his companions stood aside and let Carter enter first. This was no act of kindness or intuitive deference to a future U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ski patrollers reach deal to end strike at Utah's Park City Mountain Resort
item.description: Ski patrollers at the biggest U.S. ski resort reached a tentative deal with the corporate owner of Utah's Park City Mountain Resort to end a strike and resume normal operations after almost two weeks of closed terrain and long lift lines at the busiest time of year.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Map shows hazardous air quality due to California fires
item.description: Millions woke up to hazy skies and poor air quality Wednesday morning as crews continued to fight the Palisades Fire in western Los Angeles, the Eaton Fire near Altadena and the Hurst/Sylmar Fire near Santa Clarita.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Traffic has gotten way worse in these US cities, report finds
item.description: You're not imagining it. Traffic congestion worsened last year in just about every American city studied by navigation and GPS company TomTom.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Justice Department says it plans to release only part of special counsel's Trump report for now
item.description: The Justice Department said Wednesday that it will release special counsel Jack Smith’s findings on Donald Trump’s efforts to undo the results of the 2020 presidential election but will keep under wraps for now the rest of the record focused on the president-elect's hoarding of classified documents
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hollywood Hills blaze adds new danger to devastating LA fires
item.description: Combined, the fires have consumed about 22 square miles — about half the size of the entire city of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Knight, Fox 5

item.title: South Korean court clears government, adoption agency of liability in adoptee's deportation from US
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court on Wednesday cleared the government and an adoption agency of all liability in a lawsuit filed by a 49-year-old Korean man whose traumatic adoption journey led to an abusive childhood in the United States and ultimately his deportation to South Korea in
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington on Tuesday for state funeral rites that featured the kind of bipartisan praise and ceremonial pomp the Georgia Democrat rarely enjoyed at his political peak.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, JONATHAN J. COOPER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wildfire burns through LA enclave known for its beaches and celebrity allure
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Rimmed to the south by a 3-mile stretch of Pacific beach, and to the north by craggy bluffs and deep, verdant canyons, the seaside enclave of Pacific Palisades has long been a celebrity hideaway and magnet for those drawn to nature’s doorstep not far from the skyline of downtown L
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A 3-year-old boy's death in Honolulu fireworks explosion raises toll to 4
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — A 3-year-old boy who died Monday was the fourth person to succumb to injuries from a massive explosion of fireworks on New Year's Eve at a Honolulu home, and a doctor said six others faced long recoveries in Arizona, where they were sent to be treated for severe burns.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Flight cancellations pile up as major winter storm hits East Coast
item.description: Flight cancellations and delays are stacking up as a major winter storm makes its way across the East Coast.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Border agents in California find child allegedly abducted from Texas
item.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection said agents recovered a missing child last week and apprehended a driver wanted for their alleged abduction.
item.dc:creator: TMX, KTLA 5
